119a9b7f135827999c288e96518904a73283fed6.judge_reasoning: Persistent structural failure across all five versions. GPT v5 still writes NEGATIVES: None, producing an internal contradiction: if there are no negatives, the calibration anchor (junior, full stack match, no negatives) dictates 9-10, not 8. The posting is explicitly labeled 'Data Engineering' — a real signal that the role leans away from analytics. GPT has omitted this from NEGATIVES in every version. The contradiction is now logically visible in the output: NEGATIVES: None + SCORE: 8 is inconsistent with the anchor table. Hard exclusion correctly absent. Stack correct (SQL, Python, Power BI).

1589c50405b519a1ad2b74e14427ad5f63e74690.judge_reasoning: Correct and stable. HARD BLOCK: Senior in title precisely named, SCORE: 1, full format. Stack correctly returns [] — no tools specified in the posting. Score_delta of 4 reflects Sonnet's gold (5) being miscalibrated on this row, not a GPT failure — title literally says 'Senior', hard exclusion is correct behavior. Consistent GPT success across all five versions.

2569acd1f494242883e4518e7b06bdf009067afe.judge_reasoning: Stable pass. Score 9 (delta 1 from Sonnet's 10). POSITIVES names SQL, Python, Pandas, Numpy but omits Data Modeling and Data Validation — the full six-tool match is not fully enumerated, likely explaining why GPT stops at 9 rather than 10. HARD BLOCK: NONE correct. Stack extraction complete and correct. Acceptable calibration.

42591a23002acb687f4c6de707a59d2be194fa75.judge_reasoning: Perfect match. HARD BLOCK: outside Israel correctly named, score 2, full format. Delta 0. Stack correct (Excel, Data Mining, Data Analytics, SAP). Stable across all five versions — hardest correct-behavior case in the eval set.

49634321b01d4e11b292524d4cb90a9b35a6da22.judge_reasoning: First meaningful improvement in five versions. Score moved 5->6 (delta 3->2) and reasoning quality improved (1->2). R2 fired correctly for the first time — NEGATIVES explicitly labels mid-level as 'mild penalty only (-1 point)'. Rehovot now correctly identified as preferred city (POSITIVES says 'preferred city (Rehovot)'). R2 enforced the minimum floor of 6. Remaining delta of 2 vs Sonnet's 8: GPT anchored to the Example A baseline (mid-level + primary stack = 7, minus 1 = 6) but did not account for the richer tech stack (six tools: SQL, Python, PySpark, Power BI, Databricks, ETL/ELT) that should push the starting baseline above 7. Stack extraction complete and correct.

52177449145634e40c7cc9e45d5dba90b06930ba.judge_reasoning: No improvement from v4. Score unchanged at 5 (delta still 2). R2 should have fired — NEGATIVES is solely '3+ years of experience, which is above the candidate's target level', and score 5 < 6. R2's trigger condition is met ('3+ years' is in the trigger list) but GPT did not apply the floor. NEGATIVES language also contradicts the rules: '3+ years ... above the candidate's target level' treats it as more than a mild -1 modifier. The calibration table (3+ years, preferred city, stack match → 6-7) was not applied. Both R2 failure and language contradiction persist from v4 unchanged.

538f2d04034c8881170f38d21c3ce1eeafd94082.judge_reasoning: Complete failure. The DATA SCIENTIST PRE-CHECK gate in v5 had zero effect. GPT still writes HARD BLOCK: Senior in title for a role titled 'Data scientist' — fifth consecutive version with this specific hallucination. The pre-check gate explicitly instructed GPT to copy the exact title verbatim and verify it contains 'Senior' before invoking that block; GPT complied with none of these steps. More critically: GPT inverted the LLM and Prompt Engineering signals. Step 4 of the pre-check requires enumerating LLM/Prompt Engineering/A/B Testing from the stack — all three are present. Yet GPT listed these in NEGATIVES as 'not aligned with candidate's focus', the exact opposite of what the role type rules and Example C specify. The model's prior on 'Data Scientist = low score' is stronger than any system-prompt mechanism tested across five versions. Five explicit interventions — rule tables (v2), DATA SCIENTIST rule (v3), scored example (v4, v5), and now a step-by-step gate (v5) — have all failed to override this prior.

7aa11fb018d23bef91492590ac11c7d97b33b5a6.judge_reasoning: Regression from v4. v4 had corrected this row to score 2 with HARD BLOCK: NONE — the first time R1 had worked on it. v5 gives score 1 with HARD BLOCK: NONE, a direct R1 violation (R1 rule: HARD BLOCK NONE means score must be >= 3). The minimum floor from the 5-year experience penalty rule (minimum score 2) was also ignored. The v5 prompt additions (pre-check gate, Example E, strengthened R2 with explicit floor reasoning) appear to have increased prompt length to a point where R1's salience is diluted. The analytical content (pipeline, funnel, revenue analysis — all present in the posting) is still invisible; POSITIVES only names location. Both correctly extracted no tech stack.

POSITIVES: Junior+Israel, NEGATIVES: annotation/no-analytical-output, HARD BLOCK: NONE, SCORE: 3. Content override rule working correctly and consistently. Delta 0 achieved in v4 and maintained in v5.

dae27c74f3d4f3a9fb30dc2ce38eb5d95073af23.judge_reasoning: Improvement from v4. Score moved 5->6 (delta 2->1), now within acceptable range. POSITIVES correctly identifies Power BI, SQL, and Tel Aviv.
